title: Evaluation Datasets, Hard Negatives, Leakage, and Experiment Design
description: An evaluation set contains synthetic easy questions generated from the indexed chunks, uses the same examples to tune and test, and has no hard negatives, slices, or versioned judgments. Learners cumulatively produce versioned retrieval and answer test collection through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Sample real and designed queries across intents, languages, difficulty, freshness, answerability, access, source types, and harm with documented weights.
Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.
Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.
